sources.notes: Construct k appended values so no unwanted palindromic subarray is created.
Official editorial shows that repeating three suitable distinct values cyclically is sufficient, with small case handling.

annotation.summary: Append k values to avoid creating additional palindromic subarrays under the problem's beauty condition.
annotation.constraints: k can be large, so the construction must be periodic rather than chosen one by one.
annotation.core_idea: A length-3 cycle x,y,z,x,y,z,... with three distinct values avoids length-2 and length-3 palindromic patterns inside the appended tail. Choose the three values so they also do not interact badly with the existing suffix; if the direct last-three choice is blocked, replace one value by a spare distinct value. Then output the cycle for k steps.

solution_variants.name: period-three-construction
solution_variants.summary: Pick three safe distinct values and repeat them cyclically for all k appended positions.

tags.evidence: The solution directly constructs the appended sequence using a safe period-3 pattern.
The construction greedily chooses a small set of safe values instead of optimizing over the full sequence.
The output is a repeated schedule of three values designed to avoid local conflicts.
